properties.titulo_evento: Poland buys V-BAT drones to boost Baltic Sea naval operations
properties.descricao: The deal puts Poland in line to become the first country on NATO’s eastern flank to acquire the Shield AI-made drone.

properties.titulo_evento: White House asks Congress for $87.6 billion, mostly for Iran war
properties.descricao: The request includes $67.15 billion for the military, including $21 billion for munitions and to strengthen the U.S. industrial base.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany scraps F126 frigate program, pivots to MEKO warships amid cost and contractor chaos
properties.descricao: Germany Navy leaders have already blessed the fallback ships as capable of meeting NATO capability requirements, but an alliance deadline looms.

properties.titulo_evento: Tank maker KNDS plans stock market listing, Germany to buy 40% stake
properties.descricao: The firm showed a proposed battle tank at Eurosatory defense exhibition in Paris boasting a French turret and gun mounted on a German Leopard 2 chassis.

properties.titulo_evento: US Senate joins House in voting to halt Iran war
properties.descricao: It marks the first time both chambers approved directing a president to remove armed forces from hostilities since the War Powers Act was adopted in 1973.

properties.titulo_evento: Frustrating Israel, fiber-optic killer drone technology has arrived in southern Lebanon
properties.descricao: Analysts believe that any military engaged in conflict will, in one form or another, encounter technologies first seen in Ukraine.

properties.titulo_evento: US says Iran will buy its goods: What could US-Iran trade look like?
properties.descricao: Before the 1979 revolution in Iran, Washington and Tehran had close trade ties. Could a peace deal revive them?

properties.titulo_evento: In pictures: Aftermath of twin earthquakes in Venezuela
properties.descricao: Communities in northern Venezuela struggle after deadly quakes as rescuers race against time to find survivors.

properties.titulo_evento: World reacts as devastating Venezuela earthquakes kill at least 32
properties.descricao: Global leaders express solidarity with Venezuela as dual earthquakes kill at least 32 and leave widespread devastation.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran warns against Hormuz crossings without authorisation
properties.descricao: An oil tanker navigates the strait, reportedly hugging the western coast, despite threats from Revolutionary Guards.

properties.titulo_evento: Moments of panic as earthquakes strike Venezuela
properties.descricao: Video captured the first moments of two earthquakes striking Venezuela, triggering panic as people fled for safety.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran war day 118: IRGC rejects new Hormuz route; Rubio to meet GCC leaders
properties.descricao: Iran's Revolutionary Guard warns against alternative route in Strait of Hormuz as top US diplomat to meet Gulf leaders.

properties.titulo_evento: Rescuers pull survivors from rubble after Venezuela earthquakes
properties.descricao: A major search-and-rescue effort is underway in Venezuela after two earthquakes levelled buildings and left many trapped

properties.titulo_evento: Venezuela rocked by 7.5, 7.2 earthquakes: What happened and what we know
properties.descricao: Rescue efforts under way as Venezuela declares a state of emergency after powerful twin quakes cause widespread damage.

properties.titulo_evento: In pictures: Venezuela hit by twin earthquakes
properties.descricao: Caracas airport closed, metro suspended and power outages reported after deadly quakes rock Venezuela.

properties.titulo_evento: Thousands feared dead as twin earthquakes cause devastation across Venezuel
properties.descricao: Thousands feared dead as twin earthquakes cause devastation across Venezuela.

properties.titulo_evento: Iranians mark first Ashura since Khamenei’s killing in the US-Iran war
properties.descricao: Thousands gathered in Tehran on the eve of Ashura, the first since the killing of Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei.

properties.titulo_evento: 'I thought I was going to die' - Venezuelans describe earthquake panic
properties.descricao: Buildings were flattened in the capital Caracas, where voices have been heard calling from the rubble.

properties.titulo_evento: Trump asks Congress for billions for Iran war, after tension with Republicans
properties.descricao: But the budget faces an uphill battle as the president spars with some members of his own party over the issue.

properties.titulo_evento: Oil price falls to levels not seen since before Iran war
properties.descricao: Energy prices have been on a wild ride since Iran responded to US and Israeli attacks by effectively closing the Strait of Hormuz.

properties.titulo_evento: Clean sweep for Mamdani-backed candidates in New York's Democratic primary
properties.descricao: Brad Lander unseats Dan Goldman in a race that laid bare the city's divisions over the Israel-Gaza war.

properties.titulo_evento: Biggest city in Crimea without power after Ukraine strikes
properties.descricao: Sevastopol's Moscow-installed governor warns there will be no electricity in some areas until the evening.

properties.titulo_evento: UN nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran sites as part of war deal
properties.descricao: Rafael Grossi says the IAEA is "working on modalities" but an Iranian  minister says such access would only be part of a final deal with the US.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany news: Deutsche Bahn advises against travel amid heat
properties.descricao: Germany's national rail operator is allowing passengers to cancel trips because of the heat. Meanwhile, as Germany prepare for their next World Cup match, in-form striker Deniz Undav will stay on the bench. DW has more.

properties.titulo_evento: Why is Germany taking a big stake in a major tankmaker?
properties.descricao: The German government will buy a 40% stake in tankmaker KNDS. The deal is as much about control in the critical defense sector as it is about the Franco-German relationship.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran: Rubio says US rejects any nation's claim over Hormuz
properties.descricao: Marco Rubio says the US will not accept that the Strait of Hormuz belongs to any country. Meanwhile, Iran's Revolutionary Guard warns against any crossings of Hormuz without authorization. Follow DW for more.

properties.titulo_evento: US: Trump feuds with Senators after rare Iran war rebuke
properties.descricao: US President Donald Trump abruptly canceled a housing bill signing ceremony and clashed with certain Senators after a rare motion seeking to rein in his war with Iran. The Senate passed a new motion following the feud.

properties.titulo_evento: Venezuela: 164 dead in devastating, back-to-back earthquakes
properties.descricao: The acting president has reported at least 164 deaths in Venezuela after two strong earthquakes rattled the capital and surrounding regions.

properties.titulo_evento: Venezuela hit by two powerful earthquakes
properties.descricao: Strong earthquakes struck west of Venezuela's capital just seconds apart, bringing down buildings in Caracas.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany's Merz rallies European NATO allies ahead of summit
properties.descricao: Chancellor Friedrich Merz hosted leaders of European NATO powers in Berlin to hash out consensus on bolstering European defense. With US tensions looming, the leaders signaled that transatlantic ties are improving.

properties.titulo_evento: What does British PM's resignation mean for European security?
properties.descricao: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer was widely known for his support of Ukraine and his efforts towards a new EU–UK defense partnership. What will happen to those policies now that Starmer has announced his resignation?

properties.titulo_evento: Iran after the war — fragile peace, shattered economy
properties.descricao: The US has temporarily allowed Iran to export oil and indicated it will provide billions to relieve the crisis triggered by the war. With ordinary Iranians at their limit, however, Tehran already fears new protests.

properties.titulo_evento: Climate change: Heat wave costs the German economy billions
properties.descricao: Never before has a June been as hot in Germany as in 2026. This has consequences not only for people and nature, but also for businesses. Heat reduces productivity and causes illness, while cooling is expensive.

properties.titulo_evento: Trump offers Iran an economic lifeline — with strings
properties.descricao: Iran has seen a speedy rebound in oil exports after the US-Iran deal granted immediate waivers on US sanctions. Relief on frozen assets and a $300 billion reconstruction fund now hinge on Tehran meeting key conditions.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany news: Major warship project to be scrapped
properties.descricao: The Defense Ministry is dropping a multi-billion-euro project to build F126 frigates, media say. And train services have resumed after a communications glitch overnight halted Germany's rail network . DW has more.

properties.titulo_evento: European leaders pledge stronger role in NATO before Trump-Rutte talks
properties.descricao: The E5 group also called for Ukraine aid to rank high on the agenda at the upcoming  alliance summit in Ankara.

properties.titulo_evento: Lesson Learned From Iran War: Reduce Reliance on Strait of Hormuz
properties.descricao: Increased stockpiles, more pipelines and other tactics could loosen the waterway’s stranglehold on the global economy.

properties.titulo_evento: Oil Prices Hover Around Prewar Levels as Gulf Shipping Resumes
properties.descricao: Oil prices fell, moving closer to levels not seen since the war in Iran began in February.

properties.titulo_evento: A.I. Riches Fuel Economic Divide in Asia’s Chip Powerhouses
properties.descricao: A.I. demand is driving stock market gains and booming exports in South Korea and Taiwan. But the rest of the economy is being left behind.

properties.titulo_evento: Alibaba Sues Pentagon Over China Military Label
properties.descricao: The Chinese tech giant said it had no ties to China’s military and that the U.S. government had violated the law by making that claim.
